regions.us-ports: Today, Miami, the primary US cruise homeport, is experiencing clear skies with a south wind at 6 knots and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions. Port Everglades, Port Canaveral, and Tampa Bay also expect favorable conditions with SSE winds at 14 knots and partly cloudy skies, maintaining less than 10% rain probability, which should keep embarkation and excursions running on schedule. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Miami will see a shift to a southerly wind at 10 knots with continued partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, while Port Everglades and Tampa Bay will experience similar conditions, ensuring minimal disruption. Beyond 48 hours, Miami faces a SSE wind at 12 knots accompanied by drizzle but still less than 10% rain probability, which may slightly affect outdoor activities but should not impact overall operations. Other major ports including Port Canaveral and Tampa Bay will monitor these trends closely for any necessary adjustments.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today in Nassau and the surrounding Bahamas ports including Freeport, Bimini, and Berry Islands, winds are light from the northeast at 2 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift to the southeast at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 2% rain chance in Nassau, while Key West and Grand Cayman will see similar conditions with light southeast winds and minimal rain probability, allowing normal cruise operations. By day three, wind speeds increase to 13 knots from the southeast accompanied by drizzle and only a 1% chance of rain, which may slightly reduce visibility but will not impact port activities in Ocho Rios, Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, or Samaná. There are no Small Craft Advisories or higher wind warnings in effect, so we anticipate steady marine conditions across the Central Caribbean and Bahamas through the period. Passengers and crew should continue normal operations with routine monitoring for any updates.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today in San Juan, winds are light from the southeast at 5 knots with clear skies and only a 2% chance of rain, allowing for smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds will increase to 13 to 17 knots with drizzle expected and rain chances remaining below 30 percent, so ports including St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. Maarten should experience minor weather impacts without operational disruptions. By day three, winds will ease slightly to 15 knots from the east, but a 30% rain probability may affect shore excursions in St. Kitts and Antigua, requiring some caution for outdoor activities. No marine wind warnings are in effect, so cruise ship operations across the Eastern Caribbean ports should remain stable throughout this period.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, southeast winds at 10 knots and overcast skies combined with a 47% rain chance may affect shore excursions and embarkation operations in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City, so plan accordingly for possible delays or wet conditions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds steady at 10 knots and drizzle with a 66% rain chance are expected to impact all four ports, making wet conditions likely and potentially affecting outdoor activities and port transfers. Beyond 48 hours, southeast winds increasing to 13 knots with a 41% chance of drizzle may affect operations in the region, though impacts should be lighter compared to day two. Current visibility is good with clear skies and light southeast winds at 8 knots in Cozumel, allowing safe vessel maneuvering this morning. Crew and passengers should monitor announcements for updates as conditions evolve.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated easterly winds sustained at 17 knots with overcast skies are present in Aruba, supporting steady port operations and normal embarkation procedures with no rain expected. In Curacao and Bonaire, similar east winds near 20 knots and mostly cloudy skies will maintain consistent conditions for shore excursions and docking today. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Aruba will see sustained east winds near 19 to 20 knots with drizzle and rain chances rising slightly to 6-8 percent, while Cartagena will also experience east winds around 18-20 knots with minimal rain probability below 10 percent, keeping impacts minimal. Beyond 48 hours, these elevated east winds near 19 knots with continued drizzle in Aruba and Curacao suggest stable but breezy conditions, with rain chances remaining under 10 percent, indicating ongoing smooth cruise vessel operations and shore activity. Passengers and crew should remain aware of steady east winds around 20 knots across the Southern Caribbean ports, which may cause moderate sea states but no significant disruption is forecast.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, winds out of the east-northeast at 7 knots and partly cloudy skies across Barbados support smooth port operations and shore excursions with no rain expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, the Lesser Antilles, including St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten, will see winds steady at 10 knots from the east-northeast with overcast skies on day one and a 6% rain chance, and light drizzle with a 2% rain chance on day two, posing no operational concerns. For St. Vincent and Grenada, expect east-northeast winds around 10 knots with similar low rain probabilities, ensuring continued favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to east at 11 knots with drizzle increasing to a 14% rain probability, primarily affecting Barbados, but still below operational impact thresholds for the region. Crew and passengers should remain aware of these steady trade winds and mostly light precipitation conditions as we proceed through the week.
regions.los-angeles: Today winds out of the west-southwest at 4 knots and clear skies with 0% rain support smooth embarkation and disembarkation at Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island with no expected impacts to shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing west-southwest winds up to 11 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain, maintaining favorable conditions for all ports. By the second day, a shift to southerly winds near 10 knots will bring foggy conditions primarily affecting visibility at Marina del Rey and Catalina Island, which may require adjustments to vessel navigation and shore excursion timing. Beyond 48 hours, southerly winds will ease to 8 knots with a return to clear skies and zero rain chance, indicating stable conditions for cruise operations across the region. No marine wind warnings or weather hazards are forecast during this period.
regions.ensenada: Today, winds are light at 1 knot from the east with clear skies and no chance of rain, creating ideal conditions for smooth embarkation and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift WSW and increase to 9-10 knots with overcast skies, which will primarily impact operations at Ensenada and Punta Banda by reducing sunlight but not affecting rain-free conditions. By the third day, WSW winds will ease slightly to 8 knots while fog develops, lowering visibility around Islas Todos Santos but with no rain expected. Cruise vessels and passengers should prepare for reduced visibility during morning hours beyond 48 hours, which may require cautious navigation and possible adjustments to excursion timing.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, winds are light from the northwest at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 3% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz to proceed without weather-related interruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect west winds increasing to 8-9 knots with overcast conditions and minimal rain chances of 6% on Day 1 and 0% on Day 2; these conditions will impact all three ports with reduced sunshine but no operational concerns. By Day 3, winds shift slightly to WNW at 11 knots with persistent overcast skies and zero rain chance, which should maintain steady cruise ship maneuvering and safe embarkation/disembarkation in the region. There are no developing wind advisories or marine warnings forecasted over the next 72 hours, so cruise operations across Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz should remain stable. Passengers and crew can plan shore excursions with confidence given the low rain probabilities and moderate wind speeds throughout this period.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the north at 4 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing northwest winds at 13 knots on Day 1 and 11 knots on Day 2 with overcast skies and no rain probability, which may cause slight increases in sea state but should not affect port operations at any of the three ports. By Day 3, winds shift more west-northwest at 8 knots, remaining overcast with no rain expected, keeping conditions stable for continued cruise operations. There are no small craft advisories or warnings in effect, so normal vessel maneuvering and tendering can proceed without weather-related restrictions. Passengers and crew should prepare for mostly overcast skies and moderate northwest winds through the next three days.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in the Puerto Vallarta region, winds are light from the northeast at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, allowing smooth port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southwest winds increasing to 10 knots with overcast skies and no rain probability, which will maintain steady conditions for all ports in the area. Beyond 48 hours, the forecast continues with southwest winds steady at 10 knots and persistent overcast skies with zero percent rain chance, supporting continued normal cruise vessel maneuvering and embarkation activities. Passengers and crew should anticipate consistent wind directions and speeds through this period, ensuring no significant weather disruptions at any of the regional ports.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, an elevated rain chance of 43% with overcast skies may affect shore excursions and embarkation in Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca as easterly winds increase to 9 knots. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect fog with a 20% rain probability and light SSE winds at 7 knots around Ibiza, Malaga, and Cadiz, presenting generally favorable conditions for port operations. By day three, northeast winds strengthen to 12 knots with a 98% chance of rain showers expected to impact Gibraltar and Lisbon, likely disrupting disembarkation and ship maneuvering. Passengers and crew should prepare for increasing wet conditions and reduced visibility beyond 48 hours in the western Mediterranean.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, an 85% rain chance is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in Naples, Civitavecchia, and Genoa, with northerly winds at 5 knots contributing to damp conditions despite partly cloudy skies earlier. Current conditions in Naples show NNE winds at 4 knots and 0% rain chance, but increasing cloud cover and rain later today will likely reduce visibility and slow embarkation/disembarkation procedures. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, a 45% probability of rain may affect operations in Nice, Monaco, and Marseille as NNE winds increase to 6 knots and skies remain predominantly overcast. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to WSW at 7 knots with largely overcast conditions and only a 3% rain chance in Naples, signaling improving conditions for smoother port operations in Sardinia, Corsica, and Venice. Passengers and crew should anticipate wet weather today and possible intermittent showers tomorrow, especially in northern and western ports.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today in Athens (Piraeus) we see southwest winds at 3 knots under overcast skies with no rain expected, so port operations and shore excursions are proceeding normally. Looking ahead to the next 24 to 48 hours, a 48% rain chance is forecast with south-southeast winds increasing to 8 knots, which may affect embarkation and shore activities in Athens, Santorini, and Mykonos. Rhodes and Cyprus should also monitor conditions as similar wind shifts and rain probability increases are expected regionally. Beyond 48 hours, skies will remain mostly overcast with south-southwest winds around 6 knots and rain chances dropping to 0%, signaling improved conditions for port visits in Istanbul, Izmir, Haifa, and Alexandria. Crew and passengers should prepare for moderate wind shifts and the potential for rain in key eastern Mediterranean ports during day two.
